narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because Japan intervention, mixed US data, and fading USD CSM from 21 on D1 to 0 on H4 confirm accelerating dollar selling pressure
2. JPY cross selling because USDJPY correlation cluster above 0.91 across all JPY crosses drives synchronized yen strength as intervention narrative dominates and JPY CSM sits at 40 versus a weakening USD base
3. EUR and CHF acceleration because H4 CSM divergence shows EUR surging from 0 to 99 and CHF holding 85 to 100, signaling capital rotating into European safe and liquid currencies against the dollar

PRICE CONFIRMS

USDCHF sell signal aligning on both D1 and H4 is the cleanest confirmation of dollar weakness, supported by the negative 0.71 correlation with EURUSD which is also pressing higher on H4 buy signals. GBPUSD leads the G10 USD pairs with the strongest CSM reading at 100 and a confirmed H4 buy, while AUDUSD and NZDUSD lag with CSM readings of 47 and lower, confirming commodity currency underperformance relative to the European bloc. JPY cross sell signals on EURJPY, GBPJPY, CADJPY, and NZDJPY are internally consistent given the 0.94 to 0.97 correlation cluster, rejecting any idea of isolated yen moves.

REGIME

Mixed with Risk-On lean, conflicted. S&P 500 up 0.39 percent and Copper up 0.72 percent support risk-on, but Gold at 4632 up and VIX at 16.95 rising 0.36 percent simultaneously signal residual safe-haven demand that prevents a clean risk-on confirmation.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. DXY versus risk-on narrative: DXY is up 0.13 percent intraday while all USD CSM and fundamental signals point bearish, suggesting short-term covering noise against the dominant directional trend
2. CAD divergence: D1 CSM shows CAD strong at 91 but H4 fades to 52 and CADJPY prints a sell signal, while WTI oil dropping 3 percent undermines the commodity currency bid that elevated CAD in the first place

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF down - dual timeframe sell signal, EUR and CHF CSM surging, USD collapsing - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: GBPUSD up - GBP CSM at 100, H4 buy confirmed, USD weakest currency on board - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: AUDUSD - AUD CSM at 47 is weakest among risk pairs, WTI oil drop adds headwind
Watch: DXY breaking below 98.00 on H4 close confirms USD selling accelerates and activates primary trade with full conviction

themes.theme: US-Iran Peace Talks Reduce Risk Premium
Trump Tariff Threats on European Vehicles
Mixed US Economic Data Limits Dollar

key_observation: USD weakening significantly driven by Japan intervention in USD/JPY and reduced geopolitical risk premium from Iran peace talks. DXY hitting two-week lows. Risk-on sentiment supported by equity rallies and commodity strength offsetting tariff concerns.
watch: NFP data release next week, continuation of Japan intervention, US-Iran negotiations progress, Trump tariff implementation on EU vehicles, USD/JPY technical levels post-intervention, DXY support near two-week lows
